item.title: Investment group EQT submits ‘final’ £10.6bn offer for Intertek
item.description: FTSE 100 testing group comes under mounting pressure from shareholders to engage with buyout firm

item.title: Japanese snack maker goes black and white on Iran war supply crunch
item.description: Calbee to drain colour from packaging in response to rising prices of petroleum-based inks

item.title: UAE gas plant hit by Iranian attacks will not be fully repaired until 2027
item.description: Damage to Habshan facility highlights lasting impact of Middle East conflict on Gulf energy exports

item.title: Two ex-Carillion finance directors fined by accountancy regulator
item.description: FRC wraps up outstanding investigation into 2018 collapse of outsourcing group

item.title: Koreans should share companies’ AI windfall, says presidential adviser
item.description: Samsung and SK Hynix stocks dip after policy chief comments

item.title: Thinking globally systemic, acting locally systemic
item.description: The revenge of ‘glocal’

item.title: FirstFT: Starmer on the brink
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Warsh’s Fed in-tray and Iran truce on ‘life support’

item.title: Peltz in talks to raise funds for Wendy’s go-private bid
item.description: The move is a value play for the hamburger chain whose shares have slid 70% in the past 5 years

item.title: The Gulf’s dealmaking machine sputters
item.description: Bankers were on track for a bumper year until war broke out in February

item.title: Why personalised pricing could be a good deal for shoppers
item.description: Selling the same thing to different people at a different price sounds unfair, but it needn’t be bad news for consumers

item.title: US communications regulator targets Chinese tech for security risks
item.description: FCC chair Brendan Carr cracks down on goods from drones to routers despite trade thaw with Beijing

item.title: The UK strategy for the City needs more ambition
item.description: A hyperactive reform programme is not enough to drive the urgent national mission of driving growth and investment

item.title: Will AI turn us all into hipsters and artisans?
item.description: There is good reason to be dubious about the notion that automation will supplant all demand for human labour

item.title: France presses EU to crack down on platforms like Shein and Temu
item.description: Consumer body warns that platforms are selling unsafe items including overheating hairdryers and exploding batteries

item.title: Oil majors return to Alaska as state becomes ‘world’s hottest play’
item.description: Exxon and Shell among those resuming Arctic exploration in Trump’s ‘drill, baby, drill’ era

item.title: Hedge funds bet on biofuels to profit from Iran oil price shock
item.description: Traders expect corn and soyabeans to soar as demand for alternative fuel sources rises

item.title: HSBC convenes meeting of UK banks over climate risk disclosure demands
item.description: Central banks have warned that floods, wildfires and rising sea levels could trigger defaults among household and corporate borrowers

item.title: Why DS Smith’s global cardboard box merger fell apart
item.description: International Paper is quickly reversing a transatlantic takeover in a century-old industry

item.title: How war hit the Gulf dealmaking machine
item.description: Bankers had been on pace for $1bn in deal fees for the first time in almost 20 years. Now, everything is on ice

item.title: The boss of Italy’s defence champion fell foul of politics — and the army
item.description: Investors loved Roberto Cingolani’s high-tech strategy for Leonardo. The military establishment was less convinced.

item.title: The ranking: Africa’s Fastest-Growing Companies 2026
item.description: South Africa dominates list as Kenya leapfrogs Nigeria into second place and Egyptian business tops the ranking for first time

item.title: Amazon staff use AI tool for unnecessary tasks to inflate usage scores
item.description: In-house MeshClaw tool enables employees to delegate jobs to AI agents and climb company’s AI leaderboard

item.title: Will investors embrace China’s humanoid robot champion?
item.description: Unitree aims to go public later this year in a crucial test for android industry

item.title: Lotus offers lifeline to troubled UK car plant
item.description: Chief executive slashes sales target and unveils strategy to revive struggling manufacturer controlled by China’s Geely

item.title: BYD and peers make strides in every market but their own
item.description: Beijing is switching its attention to newer sectors such as AI and robotics

item.title: Nadella says the attempt to remove Altman from OpenAI was ‘amateur city’
item.description: Microsoft chief explains his decision to back AI lab’s boss in 2023 coup attempt during testimony in Elon Musk’s lawsuit

item.title: Hudson River generates $6.4bn in first-quarter trading revenue
item.description: Volatile markets fuelled by Iran war help to propel proprietary trading firm

item.title: US to consider suspending petrol tax as Iran war pushes up prices
item.description: Republican Senator Josh Hawley to introduce legislation after Donald Trump backs move

item.title: UK to take full ownership of British Steel
item.description: Government has been locked in talks with Chinese group Jingye over lossmaking business

item.title: Adviser fees hit $80mn and counting in Spirit Airlines bankruptcy
item.description: Current liquidation and prior restructuring procedure have cost more than $110mn combined
